snippet: The Ministerial Order 1413 – Work Experience Arrangements (PDF) External Link sets out in detail the responsibilities and duties which are outlined here. Principals may nominate a person or class of persons (‘nominated person’) employed at the school to perform the powers and functions of the principal which relate to work experience (WE) arrangements. When nominating a person, a principal must ensure that the person has the required knowledge and understanding of obligations in the Education and Training Reform Act 2006 (Vic) External Link and Ministerial Order 1413 (PDF) External Link relating to work experience arrangements. Determining who is selected as the nominated person and for what duration is up to the discretion of the principal. Examples of who nominated persons might be include, but are not limited to: If the WE coordinator is the nominated person, they must not review and approve any arrangement that they have prepared in their capacity as a WE coordinator. This ensures that the approval process for the WE arrangements is robust. In such situations, the arrangement form should be signed by the principal or another nominated person.
